summary: Frontend-only smoke + functional testing of THE SELECT TRADERS landing page (v1). All requested flows pass: page mount, all 13 sections render, sticky nav blur on scroll, smooth scroll to anchors, Hero CTA labels & Recharts chart render, 5 Product Suite rows with Community Battles 'Coming Soon' pill, Pricing Free ($0) + Pro ($49) with 'Most Popular' badge & gold border/glow, FAQ accordion expands, Community Battles email form shows error toast on empty/invalid and success toast on valid input (input clears), live ticker visible, mobile hamburger menu opens at 375x800 with all 5 nav items. Zero console errors.

issues: Source text is 'Most Popular' (rendered uppercase via CSS). Cosmetic only - matches design language. No action required unless E1 prefers literal uppercase string for SEO/screen-reader semantics.

critical_code_review_comments: ProductDeepDive.jsx is ~313 lines and packs 5 product sections + email form + 2 helper components. Approaching the file-size threshold; consider splitting per-product subcomponents into /components/landing/products/ for maintainability in future iterations.
Community Battles email form has no backend persistence (intentional in v1 per PRD). Captured emails are lost on submit - flag for a future iteration to wire to /api/notify-me or similar.
Email validation is a simple includes('@') check. Acceptable for v1 client-side gating, but recommend a stricter regex or HTML5 type=email validation message for production.
No data-testid on HowItWorks, Mission, Problem, Terms sections root - not in the request spec but worth adding for future regression coverage.
test_credentials: N/A - landing page has no auth
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing page v1 is stable. All 13 sections render, no console errors at desktop (1920x1080) or mobile (375x800). When backend integration begins (Hero live data, email capture API, auth), retest those flows with real API calls. Community Battles email form currently has NO backend call - it's a MOCKED client-side toast only.
